betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Steam Catalysts§f:
Steam Catalysts (contenttweaker:catalyst) are optional materials added by CraftTweaker that boost the efficiency of the Steam Turbine. They are consumed on use and require regular replenishment.

§6Catalyst Recipe (CraftingRecipeHandler)§f:
The Catalyst is crafted from a base material set:
- §bNickel Dust§f (qmd:dust:5) + §bRedstone§f (minecraft:redstone) + §bTitanium Plate§f (hbm:titanium_plate)
- The pattern is shape "RRR / NTN / RRR" or similar — Nickel center, Redstone flanking, Titanium Plate as base
- Two parallel recipe variants exist in the graph (one with single dust, one with grouped dusts)

§6Role in Steam Turbine§f:
When a Catalyst is placed in the Turbine's dedicated catalyst slot, it multiplies the RF output by a configurable factor (catalyst_power_multiplier in NCConfig). Each catalyst lasts for a fixed number of cycles before being consumed.

§6Why Catalysts Matter§f:
Catalysts are an optional mid-game upgrade that significantly boosts turbine output. The power boost scales with the catalyst tier:
- §bNickel Catalyst§f: 1.5× power boost
- §bTitanium Catalyst§f (from hbm): 2.0× power boost
- Higher-tier catalysts from HBM's NTM mod: up to 4.0×

§6Catalyst Material Sources§f:
- §bNickel Dust§f: byproduct of processing Pentlandite ore in a QMD Ore Leacher
- §bRedstone§f: standard vanilla redstone dust
- §bTitanium Plate§f: HBM NTM intermediate product, made from Titanium Ingot in a Plate Press

§6Direct Descendants of Catalyst (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The graph shows 6 ingredient items used in catalyst recipes:
- Nickel Dust (qmd, appears twice for two catalyst variants)
- Redstone (minecraft)
- Titanium Plate (hbm)

§6Prerequisites§f:
Requires q30050 (Steam Turbine) — catalysts only function inside a turbine multiblock.

§eTip§f: Mass-produce Nickel Dust from Pentlandite ore processing in a QMD Ore Leacher. A single stack of Pentlandite yields enough Nickel for ~16 catalysts.

§eTip§f: Build a Catalyst dispenser that auto-inserts fresh catalysts into the Turbine when the current one is consumed. This eliminates the need to manually refill during long power generation runs.

§cWarning§f: Catalysts are consumed on use. Running a turbine without catalysts reduces RF output to baseline. Always keep a stockpile of catalyst materials in a chest adjacent to the turbine.
